name: Carriage 6 — The Sleeper
summary: The rearmost passenger carriage, where you came back into yourself with a ticket in your hand and no memory of buying it.

notable_features: A luggage rack runs the length of the ceiling. The bags on it have settled into the wood — the leather has taken the shape of the brackets, not the other way around. None of them are tagged.
The rear door has no handle on this side. The keyhole is on the outside. When the train banks, cold air comes through the seam and the seam is not where the seam should be.
A route map is varnished to the panelling. The departure column reads in clear black ink; the destination column has been sanded smooth, as if someone took a long time over it.
Your own seat keeps the dent of how you were sleeping. A torn ticket stub is wedged into the cushion's piping, printed on one half, the other half clean.
The window beside the sleeping old man fogs slightly with each of his exhales. The pane is cold to the touch even where the fog has been.

san_triggers.trigger_text: The destinations on the route map have not faded. They have been removed.
The old man breathes once. Then the wheels count to sixty. Then he breathes again.
Your reflection in the window finishes turning its head a beat after you do.

skill_check_hooks.action_hint: listen to the old man's mumbling

puzzle.description: Recover the torn ticket and notice enough about the carriage to be sure you can step out of it.
puzzle.unlock_condition: Player retrieves the torn-ticket item AND succeeds a Spot Hidden (regular) on the seat OR a Listen (regular) on the old man's mumbling
